rationale: FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 only intercepts when destination size is compile-known via __builtin_object_size(). malloc-derived destinations defeat that (object size = unknown). The strcpy passes through unchecked. Axis-6 verdict policy DOES emit NOT_EXPLOITABLE for any unbounded-write where the call is in _FORTIFIED_WRITE_CALLS and FORTIFY>=2 — this is a documented OVER-suppression case: FORTIFY can't actually help here. Verdict will WRONGLY suppress; fixture documents the over-suppression edge.
